body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule` **Service directory:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/` **Triggered by:** PR #57188 by @azure-sdk (co-authored by @asifmammadov) ## What Changed PR #57188 added a significant new API for the `2026-03-01-preview` API version: - **`VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`** — a new execute-type operation for on-demand flexible VM provisioning - New models: `ComputeScheduleFlexProperties`, `ComputeSchedulePriorityProfile`, `ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile`, `ComputeScheduleZoneAllocationPolicy`, `ComputeScheduleZonePreference`, `ExecuteCreateFlexContent`, `CreateFlexResourceOperationResult` - New enums: `ComputeScheduleAllocationStrategy`, `ComputeScheduleDistributionStrategy`, `ComputeScheduleOSType`, `ComputeSchedulePriorityType` ## Gaps Found - **No code sample for `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`** — the new Flex VM creation API is mentioned in the README intro but no code example exists showing how to call it with `ComputeScheduleFlexProperties`, priority profiles, VM size profiles, and allocation strategies - **Examples section lacks inline code** — the README Examples section only links to a generic Azure SDK samples page; no actual code snippets demonstrate package-specific operations (start, deallocate, hibernate, or the new Flex create) - **Key concepts section is a stub** — the `Key concepts` section contains only a link to general Azure SDK docs; it does not explain the Flex provisioning model, the distinction between Submit vs. Execute operations, or the newly introduced concepts (priority types, VM size profiles, zone allocation policies) - **No Samples test file for `ExecuteCreateFlex`** — `tests/Samples/` has no file covering this new operation; the only scenario coverage is in `tests/Scenario/ComputescheduleOperationsTests.cs`, which is not surfaced in the README via snippet placeholders
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` — **edit** Replace the `Key concepts` section with substantive content: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### Operation types **Execute Type Operations** run immediately on targeted virtual machines. The `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` operation (added in `2026-03-01-preview`) belongs to this group and provisions VMs on demand. **Submit Type Operations** schedule an action up to 14 days in the future (e.g., `VirtualMachinesSubmitStart`, `VirtualMachinesSubmitDeallocate`, `VirtualMachinesSubmitHibernate`). ### Flex provisioning model (`VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`) The Flex create operation supports: - **Priority types** (`ComputeSchedulePriorityType`): `Spot` for preemptible capacity at reduced cost, or `Regular` for standard on-demand capacity. - **VM size profiles** (`ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile`): An ordered list of VM sizes to try when provisioning; the service works through the list until a size is successfully allocated. - **Allocation strategy** (`ComputeScheduleAllocationStrategy`): Controls whether to use `LowestPrice`, `CapacityOptimized`, or `PriceCapacityOptimized` when selecting from the VM size profiles. - **Zone preferences** (`ComputeScheduleZonePreference`): Optionally specify preferred availability zones and a zone allocation policy. - **OS type** (`ComputeScheduleOSType`): `Windows` or `Linux`. ``` Replace the `Examples` section to include a snippet placeholder for the new Flex operation in addition to a pointer to the samples page: ```markdown ## Examples The following example shows how to use `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` to provision a VM with Spot priority and multiple fallback sizes. ```C# Snippet:ComputeScheduleExecuteCreateFlex ``` Additional code samples are available on the [Azure SDK for .NET samples page](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Create** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_ExecuteCreateFlex.cs` The file should contain a test class with a method demonstrating `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex`. Wrap the core sample code in `#region Snippet:ComputeScheduleExecuteCreateFlex` / `#endregion`. Any assertions, cleanup, or test-specific infrastructure that should not appear in the README must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule; using Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule.Tests.Samples { public class Sample_ExecuteCreateFlex : SamplesBase { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ExecuteCreateFlexSample() { #region Snippet:ComputeScheduleExecuteCreateFlex ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string location = "eastus"; var flexProperties = new ComputeScheduleFlexProperties( vmSizeProfiles: new List { new ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile("Standard_D2s_v3"), new ComputeScheduleVmSizeProfile("Standard_D4s_v3"), }, osType: ComputeScheduleOSType.Linux) { PriorityProfile = new ComputeSchedulePriorityProfile { PriorityType = ComputeSchedulePriorityType.Spot, AllocationStrategy = ComputeScheduleAllocationStrategy.CapacityOptimized, }, }; var content = new ExecuteCreateFlexContent( flexProperties: flexProperties, virtualMachineResourceIds: new List { new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/my-rg/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/my-vm"), }, correlationId: Guid.NewGuid().ToString()); SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource( new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")); Response result = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlexAsync( location: location, content: content); foreach (var resourceOperation in result.Value.Results) { Console.WriteLine($"VM: {resourceOperation.ResourceId}, Status: {resourceOperation.OperationStatus}"); } #endregion } } } ``` After writing the file, add the snippet placeholder to the README as shown in Step 1. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` currently has all required sections. The following need to be enhanced: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started | ✅ Complete | No change needed | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Stub | Replace with substantive content (see Step 1) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline code | Add snippet placeholder for `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete | No change needed | | Next steps | ✅ Complete | No change needed | | Contributing | ✅ Complete | No change needed | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 computeschedule` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects code into README.md placeholder blocks; inspect the git diff to confirm the README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 computeschedule` — not needed here as no public API changed, but run to confirm no regressions 5. `dotnet format sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/` — ensures consistent formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [ComputeSchedule] Docs: Add examples and key concepts for VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex API
